entries.title: The Ensigns of Command makes Sheliak legalism the main obstacle

context.label: TNG season 3: The Ensigns of Command

continuity.sourceBoundary: Star Trek: The Next Generation season 3, Memory Alpha story rows 1-26, runtime episode aliases 1-26, primary on-screen Prime continuity.
continuity.notes: Draft reference deck. Excludes later TNG seasons, TNG films, DS9, Voyager, Picard-era material, beta canon, and fanon unless a card is an explicit future guard.

content.fact: The Sheliak insist on strict treaty terms and view the Tau Cygna V colonists as a legal removal problem rather than a humanitarian exception.
content.injection: Use the Sheliak as precise, treaty-bound legal adversaries. Picard must win through contract language, not emotional appeal.
constraints: Keep this card inside TNG season 3 unless another active Star Trek deck provides a later Context window.
Use the episode and season Context gates before injecting this fact.

entries.title: The Enemy forces Worf to confront Romulan hatred through a blood-donation request

continuity.sourceBoundary: Star Trek: The Next Generation season 3, Memory Alpha story rows 1-26, runtime episode aliases 1-26, primary on-screen Prime continuity.
continuity.notes: Draft reference deck. Excludes later TNG seasons, TNG films, DS9, Voyager, Picard-era material, beta canon, and fanon unless a card is an explicit future guard.

content.fact: Worf refuses to donate compatible blood to a dying Romulan because Romulans killed his parents, exposing deep unresolved trauma and prejudice.
content.injection: After The Enemy, Worf can carry explicit Romulan hatred tied to family loss. Do not yet import full Khitomer or Kurn revelations before Sins of The Father.

entries.title: The Defector uses Jarok defection to expose Romulan deception and tragedy

continuity.sourceBoundary: Star Trek: The Next Generation season 3, Memory Alpha story rows 1-26, runtime episode aliases 1-26, primary on-screen Prime continuity.

content.fact: Admiral Jarok defects believing he can prevent war, but the Romulans use his information as a trap and leave him personally ruined.
content.injection: Use Jarok as a tragic Romulan defector gate. His knowledge is sincere, but the larger situation is Romulan strategic deception.

content.fact: The Continuum strips Q of power and dumps him on the Enterprise, forcing the crew to deal with him as a vulnerable person instead of an omnipotent intruder.
content.injection: Use Deja Q for mortal-Q scenes only inside and after this episode. Do not make Q permanently powerless afterward.

content.fact: Sending Enterprise-C back to Narendra III restores the history where Klingons view the sacrifice as honorable and the Federation is not at war with them.
content.injection: Use Enterprise-C sacrifice as the reason the normal Federation-Klingon peace path matters. Do not keep the war timeline active after the repair.
